name: Fauna
description: Serverless document-relational database with global distribution and strict serializability (service ending May 2025)

license.comment: Core database is proprietary; company announced plans to open-source after shutdown

hosting-options.comment: Fauna is a fully managed serverless database; service ending May 30, 2025

initial-release.comment: Company founded 2012 by ex-Twitter engineers; FaunaDB came to market in 2017

primary-language.comment: Runs on the JVM as a JAR file; requires JDK 17

data-model.comment: Multi-paradigm indexed document store supporting relational, document, graph, and key-value access patterns

topology.comment: Active-active architecture with deterministic transaction processing via Calvin protocol

sharding.comment: Automatic partitioning across cluster with shared-nothing design

replication-factor.comment: Data replicated across locations within a region group

multi-region.comment: Global distribution across multiple regions in a region group with strong consistency

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency; uses Calvin protocol for globally consistent transactions

consistency-model.comment: Strict serializability for read-write transactions with serializable indexes; snapshot isolation for read-only queries

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions using the Calvin distributed transaction protocol

transaction-scope.comment: Global transactions across all replicas with single-phase commit

isolation-levels.comment: Serializable for read-write with serialized indexes; snapshot isolation for read-only and non-serialized index reads

distributed-transactions.comment: Calvin-based deterministic transaction processing across all nodes

query-language.comment: Fauna Query Language (FQL) as primary interface; GraphQL also supported

sql-compatibility.comment: Uses FQL, a TypeScript/Python-inspired expression language; no SQL support

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports B+Tree and hash table indexes with pagination

joins.comment: Native joins via Ref() type; supports hash join and sort-merge join operations

max-cluster-size.comment: Serverless architecture; cluster size abstracted from users

write-throughput.comment: No public benchmark figures available for Fauna

read-latency-p99.comment: No public latency benchmarks available; claims low latency due to single-phase commit

auto-scaling.comment: Fully serverless with automatic scaling; no capacity planning required

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic load distribution in serverless architecture

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Fully managed service with zero downtime maintenance

point-in-time-recovery.comment: MVCC with configurable data retention enables temporal queries

backup-strategy.comment: Managed backup as part of the service

kubernetes-operator.comment: No Kubernetes operator as Fauna is only available as a managed service

rust-driver.comment: No official Rust driver; community drivers may exist but not maintained

go-driver.comment: Official Go driver for Fauna v10

python-driver.comment: Official Python driver for Fauna v10

ruby-driver.comment: Community-maintained Ruby driver; not officially supported

orm-support.comment: No traditional ORM support; FQL is the primary interface

open-source.comment: Currently proprietary; company announced plans to open-source after service shutdown

free-tier.comment: Generous free tier for prototyping and small apps; note service ending May 2025

pricing-model.comment: True serverless pay-per-use pricing; scales to zero when not in use

cost-estimate-small.comment: Costs scale with usage; can be very low for light workloads. Service ending May 2025

github-stars.comment: Core database not open source; driver repos have minimal stars (fauna-js: ~50, fauna-shell: ~77)

change-data-capture.comment: Event feeds provide pull-based CDC for processing changes at your own pace

watch-subscribe.comment: Event streams provide real-time push-based subscriptions to document and collection changes

triggers.comment: Supports user-defined functions/stored procedures that can act as triggers

event-streaming.comment: Proprietary event streams and event feeds; no native Kafka/Kinesis integration
